pattern: Dynamic programming with the word set as a hash table: for each word, check whether it can be fully covered by at least two shorter dictionary words.
hints: For each candidate word, the question reduces to: can every character be covered by a sequence of at least two words from the remaining set?
A DP array of length len+1 where dp[i] is true when the first i characters form a valid concatenation; track the word count to ensure at least two parts are used.

Build a set of all words. For each word w (length L), run a DP: dp[0]=true, and for each end position j check all prefixes w[:i] that are in the set (excluding w itself) and have dp[i]=true; if dp[L] is reached with two or more parts, w is concatenated.

edge_inputs.description: classic example with three concatenated words
empty string in the list must not be treated as a building block
a word that is a repetition of itself two times
single word in the list, no concatenation possible
word can only be formed using itself, which is not allowed
multiple overlapping prefixes in the dictionary

stress.description: many short words producing a large set of concatenatable words
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    parts = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'ab', 'bc', 'ca', 'abc', 'bca', 'cab']
    word_set = set(parts)
    for _ in range(size):
        length = rng.randint(2, 4)
        w = ''.join(rng.choice(parts) for _ in range(length))
        word_set.add(w)
    return [list(word_set)]
stress.note: many short-part combinations to stress the DP
